name: Colorado Bankruptcy Law Group, LLC
description_short: Denver-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy representation. Offers full-service legal guidance from consultation through case closure.
description_long:
Colorado Bankruptcy Law Group, LLC is a Denver-based law firm focused exclusively on bankruptcy representation for individuals facing overwhelming debt. The firm serves Colorado residents seeking relief through federal bankruptcy protection, with particular emphasis on the Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 processes available under Colorado law.

The firm provides comprehensive bankruptcy services including initial consultations, financial situation analysis, pre-filing asset protection planning, petition preparation, court filing, hearing representation, and creditor negotiation. They handle documentation collection, guide clients through required credit counseling classes, and manage all interactions with the bankruptcy court, trustee, and creditors on behalf of their clients. Consultations are available via phone, video, or in-person meetings in Denver.

Colorado Bankruptcy Law Group distinguishes itself through personalized pre-filing planning designed to protect client assets within Colorado exemption limits, realistic expectations about the bankruptcy process (noting that 85-90% of Chapter 7 filers retain all assets), and continuous attorney support from initial consultation through case closure. The firm claims to have helped "hundreds of people" eliminate "millions of dollars" in debt and emphasizes that Chapter 7 cases typically resolve in approximately four months.

The firm's marketing emphasizes emotional and financial benefits (stopping creditor calls, preventing foreclosure, improving credit scores within one year) rather than legal complexity. While the website provides accurate information about Chapter 7 liquidation and exemptions, potential clients should note that actual outcomes depend heavily on individual financial circumstances, income levels, and asset valuations—factors requiring thorough evaluation during the consultation process.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Full-service representation covering all bankruptcy process stages from consultation through case closure
Multiple consultation formats available (phone, video, in-person) for accessibility
Pre-filing asset protection planning to maximize Colorado bankruptcy exemptions
Realistic statistics cited: 85-90% of Chapter 7 filers retain all assets; typical case duration of four months
Handles all creditor communication and court appearances on behalf of clients
Addresses both Chapter 7 liquidation and Chapter 13 reorganization bankruptcy options
Guides clients on post-bankruptcy credit score improvement strategies
cons: Website contains marketing language and promises (e.g., 'will change your life') that oversimplify complex legal outcomes dependent on individual circumstances
No pricing information, fee structure, or payment plan options disclosed on website
Limited transparency about specific qualifications, attorney experience, or case success rates beyond general claims
No information about alternative debt relief options or scenarios where bankruptcy may not be appropriate
Website content appears incomplete (text cuts off mid-sentence in Chapter 7 section), suggesting outdated or poorly maintained web presence
best_for: Denver-area residents with substantial unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills, personal loans) and income within Chapter 7 limits
Homeowners or vehicle owners facing foreclosure or repossession seeking immediate creditor action stoppage
Individuals earning above median income or with assets requiring Chapter 13 reorganization instead of liquidation
diagnosis: Colorado Bankruptcy Law Group is best suited for Denver-area individuals with overwhelming debt seeking Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy relief and preferring full attorney representation throughout the process. The primary caveat is that actual bankruptcy outcomes—including asset protection and timeline—depend entirely on individual income, assets, and circumstances; the firm's marketing emphasizes benefits without adequately addressing that eligibility and results require thorough financial analysis, and potential clients should independently verify all claims during consultation before committing.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy petition preparation and filing
Chapter 13 bankruptcy petition preparation and filing
Financial situation analysis and debt assessment
Pre-filing asset protection planning within Colorado exemptions
Court appearance and representation at bankruptcy hearings
Phone, video, and in-person consultation options
Documentation collection assistance (paystubs, tax returns, bank statements)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2373 Central Park Blvd Unit 100, Denver, CO 80238
